skill.name: markdown-url
skill.description: Route any website you need to visit through markdown.new by prefixing the URL for cleaner, more readable content extraction.
skill.summary: Rewrite URLs to use markdown.new proxy for cleaner markdown-friendly web content viewing.
skill.author: am-will

security_audit.summary: All 35 static findings are false positives. Network references are to the legitimate markdown.new service (core functionality). External command detection errors confused markdown code examples with shell commands. Filesystem detection misidentified URL scheme validation regex. No actual security risks present.

content.user_title: Transform Web Content to Clean Markdown
content.value_statement: Claude and Codex often struggle to extract clean text from complex web pages. This skill routes URLs through markdown.new proxy to convert any webpage into readable Markdown format for easier analysis and note-taking.
actual_capabilities: Rewrite any URL to use markdown.new proxy service
Extract clean Markdown content from documentation pages
Convert blog posts and articles to Markdown format
Provide fallback behavior when proxy fails or is blocked
Include optional CLI helper for URL conversion
Handle URL normalization for bare domains and paths
limitations: Cannot bypass sites that actively block proxy services
Does not work with authentication-required pages
Fails on sites requiring JavaScript, cookies, or session state
Cannot process local file paths or non-HTTP schemes
May be blocked by CAPTCHAs, bot detection, or paywalls

use_cases.title: Clean Documentation Reading
use_cases.description: When reading API documentation or technical guides, route the URL through markdown.new to get clean, copy-pasteable Markdown text instead of navigating complex web layouts.

use_cases.title: Blog Post Research
use_cases.description: When researching engineering blog posts or articles, extract the full content in Markdown format for easy summarization and note-taking without browser navigation.

use_cases.title: GitHub Issue Summarization
use_cases.description: When analyzing GitHub issues or pull request discussions, get clean text extraction for better understanding and quoting in responses.

prompt_templates.title: Basic URL Conversion
prompt_templates.prompt: Read the documentation at https://example.com/docs and summarize the key API endpoints.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants a quick summary of documentation

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Page Research
prompt_templates.prompt: Visit these three blog posts about React performance: https://blog1.com, https://blog2.com/react-tips, https://blog3.com/performance. Extract the main points from each and create a combined checklist.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to research across multiple sources

prompt_templates.title: GitHub Issue Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Read the GitHub issue discussion at https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/123 and extract the proposed solutions, concerns raised, and final resolution.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants structured analysis from a discussion

prompt_templates.title: Documentation Comparison
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare the authentication flows described in https://docs.lib1.com/auth and https://docs.lib2.com/auth. Extract the key differences in approach.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to compare documentation across sources

output_examples.input: Read https://example.com/api-docs and list all endpoints
output_examples.output:
I've extracted the API documentation from markdown.new. Here are the available endpoints:

- GET /users - List all users
- POST /users - Create new user
- GET /users/:id - Get user by ID
- PUT /users/:id - Update user
- DELETE /users/:id - Delete user

Based on the article content extracted via markdown.new, here are the key performance optimization points:

1. Use React.memo for expensive component renders
2. Implement virtual scrolling for long lists
3. Code-split routes using React.lazy
4. Avoid anonymous functions in JSX props
5. Use useMemo for expensive calculations

best_practices: Always try markdown.new first for documentation and blog content, then fall back to original URL if blocked
Use the markdown view for content extraction but switch to original URL for interactive elements like forms or logins
Check for block signals (401/403 errors, CAPTCHAs, empty content) and immediately fall back to original URL
Keep the markdown.new prefix in your responses so users can access the clean view themselves
anti_patterns: Do not route every URL through markdown.new - skip it for logins, payments, file uploads, or authenticated workflows
Do not retry markdown.new multiple times on failure - one attempt is enough before falling back
Do not use markdown.new for local file paths, API endpoints, or non-HTTP schemes
Do not rely on markdown.new when cookies, session state, or JavaScript execution is required

faq.question: What does markdown.new actually do?
faq.answer: markdown.new is a proxy service that converts web pages into clean Markdown format. It strips away complex layouts, ads, and navigation to provide readable text content that can be easily copied or summarized.

faq.question: When should I NOT use markdown.new?
faq.answer: Skip markdown.new for login pages, payment flows, file uploads, dashboards, or any site requiring authentication, cookies, or JavaScript interaction. Also avoid it for sites known to block proxy services.

faq.question: What happens if markdown.new fails to load?
faq.answer: If markdown.new returns errors like 401/403, shows CAPTCHAs, or has empty content, immediately fall back to the original URL. The skill includes automatic fallback behavior.

faq.question: Does this work with paywalled content?
faq.answer: No. markdown.new cannot bypass paywalls, CAPTCHAs, or bot detection. If a site gates content behind these protections, use the original URL directly.

faq.question: Can I use this for local files or non-HTTP URLs?
faq.answer: No. This skill only works with http:// and https:// URLs. It explicitly rejects file:, mailto:, and other URI schemes for security reasons.

faq.question: Is my data sent to third-party services?
faq.answer: Yes, URLs routed through markdown.new are processed by that external service. Only use this for public content you are comfortable sharing with the proxy. Avoid using it for private or sensitive URLs.
